intro: C is a procedural, manually managed language requiring explicit control over hardware, memory layout, and pointer arithmetic. Go introduces modern concurrency primitives via goroutines, automatic memory management via garbage collection, and a strict structural type system while compiling directly to machine code. Developers transition from C to Go to eliminate memory corruption bugs and drastically simplify the implementation of networked, concurrent services.

diffs.from: Weakly enforced, static typing with implicit conversions and unsafe pointer arithmetic.
diffs.to: Strongly enforced, static typing with explicit conversions only and structural interface implementation.

diffs.from: Minimalist imperative syntax requiring header files, forward declarations, and explicit pointer notation.
diffs.to: Modern, cleaner syntax with no headers, implicit variable assignment (`:=`), and automatic semicolon insertion.

diffs.from: Manual memory allocation and deallocation using `malloc`, `calloc`, and `free`, vulnerable to leaks and buffer overflows.
diffs.to: Automated memory management via a low-latency, concurrent garbage collector, paired with escape analysis.

diffs.from: No native language constructs; relies heavily on OS-level primitives like `pthread` or platform-specific libraries.
diffs.to: First-class concurrency using lightweight `goroutines` managed by the runtime and typed `channels` for communication.

diffs.from: Barebones library focused on basic I/O, math, string manipulation, and system calls.
diffs.to: Comprehensive, modern library providing out-of-the-box HTTP servers, JSON parsing, cryptography, and testing tools.

examples.title: Concurrent Execution
examples.description: Replacing raw OS threads with language-level goroutines.

examples.title: String Concatenation
examples.description: Safe heap allocations replacing dynamic array allocations and buffer copy operations.

tips.title: Shift from Pointer Arithmetic to Slices
tips.body: Go does not allow pointer arithmetic unless utilizing `unsafe.Pointer`. Instead of tracking array sizes and offsets manually via pointer increments, use Go slices which encapsulate a pointer, length, and capacity dynamically.

tips.title: Handle Errors explicitly, Don't Use Return Codes
tips.body: Abandon C integer error status patterns or `errno`. Idiomatic Go utilizes multiple return values where the last parameter is an `error` interface, forcing caller validation at the point of invocation.

tips.title: Replace Macros with Constants and Functions
tips.body: Go lacks a preprocessor. Replace C `#define` macros with typed or untyped `const` blocks, or generic functions to enforce type safety and enable debugger visibility.

tips.title: Adopt Interface Composition over Struct Nesting
tips.body: Go provides no inheritance. Replace nested C structs used for pseudo-polymorphism with flat structures that satisfy behaviors implicitly via decoupled Go `interface` definitions.
